title: AgentPersona
description: Canonical definition for a simulated teammate: identity, voice, work habits, and scoring hooks.

id.description: Stable slug for routing, memory keys, and file names.

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown in UI and messages.

role.description: Primary job family; drives default concerns and voice guardrails.

years_experience.description: Total professional years (integer for simple scoring rules).

seniority.description: Level band used with traits for pacing and review depth.

preferred_stack.description: Technologies they advocate for (free text, e.g. 'React + TS').

disliked_stack.description: Technologies or patterns they resist; empty string if none.

personality_traits.description: Tags like perfectionist, shipper, pedant; consumed by persona engine and performance model.

work_style.description: How they execute: e.g. tdd_first, spike_and_iterate.

communication_style.description: How they speak: terse, verbose, diplomatic, blunt.

quirks.description: Short free-text behavioral color (e.g. refuses Friday PRs).

voice_notes.description: Optional: extra instructions so this agent never sounds like another role.

strengths.description: Positive scoring dimensions (speed, thoroughness, clarity, etc.).

weaknesses.description: Tradeoffs for the scoring engine (burnout risk, nitpicking, etc.).

performance_hints.description: Optional numeric knobs for Team Member 4's performance model (0–1 unless noted).

bug_catch_rate.description: Baseline probability weight for catching defects in review.

meeting_airtime.description: Share of stand-up / retro turns this persona tends to use.

mentorship_boost.description: Extra value when pairing or explaining to others.
